title: next_diversifier increments DB before tx build; failed build silently skips index
description: payment.rs step 4 calls next_diversifier() which atomically reads, increments, and persists the diversifier index BEFORE the tx is built. If build_shielded_tx fails (e.g. ParamsNotLoaded, WitnessDeserialize), the diversifier was consumed but never used for a change address on-chain. The DB counter is already at current+1. The advance_diversifier_to in step 9 (best-effort) calls advance_diversifier_to(change_di+1) = advance_diversifier_to(current+1) which is a no-op (already stored). So the behavior is actually correct — each call to next_diversifier produces a unique, non-reused index even across failures. But the comment in send_payment describing the advance in step 9 as 'advance diversifier past the one used for change' is misleading: it makes it sound like step 9 does the advancing, when in fact next_diversifier already did. A contributor may 'simplify' step 9 by removing the advance_diversifier_to call, believing it is redundant, which would be harmless here but breaks the guarantee if the code is later refactored to delay the increment.
close_reason: Fixed: removed the always-no-op advance_diversifier_to call (next_diversifier already atomically advanced the DB counter). Renumbered steps 7→6 (broadcast) and 9→8 (post-broadcast) to match.
